englishDefinitions: (transitive) To disable or incapacitate; to confine to bed.
(intransitive) To recuperate or rest; to remain in a place.
(transitive) To take out of active service.
(intransitive) To go out of active service.
(transitive, basketball) To make a layup with (a basketball)
(intransitive, golf) To deliberately leave the ball further than necessary from the hole, so as to secure an easier succeeding shot.
(slang, US) To have sex; to have a sexual relationship.
persianTranslation: (گذرا) غیرفعال کردن یا ناتوان کردن. محدود شدن به رختخواب
(غیر گذرا) برای بهبودی یا استراحت; برای ماندن در مکانی
(گذری، بسکتبال) برای ساختن یک لایی آپ با (یک توپ بسکتبال)
(غیر گذرا، گلف) ترک عمدی توپ از سوراخ دورتر از آنچه لازم است، به طوری که ضربه بعدی راحت‌تر را تضمین کند.
(عامیانه، ایالات متحده) برای داشتن رابطه جنسی; برای داشتن رابطه جنسی
examples: We must lay up enough stores to get us through the winter.
He was laid up for six weeks with pneumonia.
He laid up until recovering from his illness.
She's been laid up in Reno for weeks.
The battleship is presently laid up in Portsmouth Harbour.
The cruise ship lays up in November for the winter.
He takes the pass, he drives, he lays it up and in.
Rather than try to reach the green, he chose to lay up.
